title: Define the canonical state machine and continuation rules
summary: Create the authoritative 0.6.0 contract for the canonical transaction state machine and continuation model. This ticket defines the meaning of each canonical non-terminal and terminal status, the expected checkpoint events that mark resumable progress, the role of `next_action` as the machine-readable continuation contract, the transition constraints that keep the state machine explicit and compact, and the idempotent continuation invariants that later runtime tickets must preserve when implementing interrupted verify, commit, and end handling.
outputs: A documented canonical status model for resumable transaction execution
A documented meaning for each of `in-progress`, `checking`, `verified`, `committed`, `done`, and `blocked`
A documented checkpoint-event contract for transaction continuation
A documented role for `next_action` as the primary continuation guide
A documented transition model that keeps commit non-terminal and explicit end terminal
A documented idempotent continuation policy for interrupted verify, commit, and end flows
Implementation-ready acceptance boundaries for downstream 0.6.0 tickets
acceptance_criteria: The ticket defines a single authoritative canonical status model for resumable transaction execution.
The contract explicitly defines the meaning of each canonical status: `in-progress`, `checking`, `verified`, `committed`, `done`, and `blocked`.
The contract explicitly states which statuses are terminal and which remain resumable.
The contract explicitly preserves the rule that repository commit is non-terminal and explicit end remains required for terminal success.
The contract defines the expected high-level transaction progression and any allowed repair or retry loops without making the state machine ambiguous.
The contract defines the canonical checkpoint events needed to understand continuation and interruption-safe resume behavior.
The contract explicitly defines `next_action` as the primary machine-readable continuation guide for an interrupted active transaction.

The contract defines the state-machine-level continuation meaning of interruption around verify, commit, and explicit end checkpoints without turning this ticket into the runtime resume-implementation ticket.
The contract defines idempotent continuation expectations so repeated resume of the same interrupted state does not corrupt lifecycle state or duplicate logical completion.
The contract explicitly states that planning-document status is not the canonical source of continuation truth.

The contract is specific enough that downstream tickets can implement state transitions, workflow guidance, runtime checks, and regression tests without inventing per-module continuation semantics ad hoc.
The ticket does not weaken canonical lifecycle ordering, replay integrity, resumability, or explicit terminal-state handling.
tasks: Define the canonical set of transaction statuses and the intended meaning of each status.
Define which statuses are terminal and which are resumable.
Define the expected high-level transaction progression from begin through explicit terminal completion.
Define any bounded repair or retry loops needed for non-terminal continuation after verify or commit failures.
Define the canonical checkpoint events that represent resumable lifecycle progress.
Define the relationship between checkpoint events and materialized transaction status.
Define the role of `next_action` as the primary machine-readable continuation guide for active transaction resume.
Define what `next_action` should represent for each non-terminal status and major checkpoint condition.
Define the state-machine-level continuation meaning for interruption around verify, commit, and explicit end checkpoints.
Define the idempotent continuation policy so repeated resume can safely continue the same active transaction without duplicating logical completion.
Document that transaction continuation must derive from canonical transaction state and canonical event history rather than planning-document status.

Leave implementation-ready notes for downstream tickets that will update runtime transition logic, workflow guidance, verification handling, commit handling, and interruption-focused regression coverage.
notes: This ticket is contract-definition work, not the full runtime implementation.
The most important outcome is a compact, explicit continuation model that makes interrupted transactions easy to resume correctly.
The contract should keep the state machine intentionally small and readable rather than overfitting every possible internal sub-condition into a new canonical status.
The contract should make `next_action` operationally useful enough that agents can continue interrupted work without heuristic branching.
The contract should preserve the deliberate distinction between repository commit and successful transaction completion.
The contract should keep runtime resume-selection and fallback algorithm details for later implementation tickets, especially the exact-active-transaction resume ticket.

status_meanings.in-progress: Implementation work or repair work is actively ongoing and the transaction remains resumable.
status_meanings.checking: The transaction is in a non-terminal evaluation state between active implementation work and final verified-versus-repair branching, and remains resumable.
status_meanings.verified: Verification succeeded for the current transaction state, but the transaction is still non-terminal until commit and explicit end handling are complete.
status_meanings.committed: Repository commit succeeded, but the transaction remains non-terminal until explicit terminal completion occurs.
status_meanings.done: Terminal successful completion of the transaction.
status_meanings.blocked: Terminal blocked completion of the transaction.

checkpoint_events.purpose: These events provide a compact checkpoint history sufficient to understand continuation and interruption-safe resume behavior.

continuation_contract.definition: `next_action` identifies the next canonical workflow step required to continue the active non-terminal transaction safely at the state-machine level.
continuation_contract.source_of_truth: Canonical transaction state defines the continuation contract; later implementation tickets define the exact runtime resume-entry and rebuild-selection behavior.

typical_next_actions.in-progress: Usually `tx.verify.start` once implementation or repair work reaches a safe checkpoint.
typical_next_actions.checking: Continue verification-related checking or the next required evaluation step defined by canonical workflow guidance.
typical_next_actions.verified: Usually commit if repository changes exist, otherwise move toward explicit terminal completion if policy allows.

idempotent_resume_requirements: Resuming a transaction already in `verified` must not mint a new transaction or reclassify it as new work.
Resuming a transaction already in `committed` must not duplicate logical commit completion.
Resuming a transaction already in a terminal state must not re-activate it as active work.
Repeated continuation attempts for the same interrupted state must preserve exact active transaction identity.

design_bias.preferred_anchor: Status plus `next_action` plus explicit transition constraints
avoid: using planning-document status as runtime continuation truth
treating commit success as implicit terminal success
over-expanding the canonical status model for internal implementation convenience
pulling exact resume-selection algorithm details into the state-machine contract

canonical_alignment.synchronization_intent: Use this ticket as the contract foundation for later 0.6.0 work on workflow guidance, runtime transition logic, exact active-transaction resume implementation, and regression tests.
canonical_alignment.current_canonical_constraint: Downstream implementation work should not invent ad hoc continuation semantics or extra canonical statuses before this contract is treated as the authoritative boundary, but exact runtime resume-entry and fallback behavior should remain implementation work for later tickets.
